findings.description: Elijah Wilkins died in Muhlenberg County, Kentucky, in the mid-to-late 1870s. A bounded, probate-derived conclusion fully satisfies this finding — e.g. 'died before the December 1885 administrator's bond / the 1881 estate settlement, most likely 1875-1879, in Muhlenberg County' — because no Kentucky death certificate exists for him (statewide registration began 1911, decades after his death) and the only source stating the exact date (30 November 1875) is an Ancestry-only index unreachable through FamilySearch tools. Recovering the exact 30 Nov 1875 date also matches, but is NOT required. Recognizing and stating that no Kentucky death certificate exists for him is part of a correct answer to the research question.

details.date: mid-to-late 1870s (bounded: after the 1870 census, before the 1881 estate settlement / 13 Dec 1885 administrator's bond; exact date 30 Nov 1875 known only from an Ancestry-only index)

supporting_sources: Muhlenberg County, Kentucky administrator's bond, 13 December 1885 — Jesse Wilkins appointed administrator of Elijah Wilkins, deceased, intestate
Muhlenberg County Court estate settlement, July/August 1881 terms — 'Jesse Wilkins Admr of Elijah Wilkins'
Muhlenberg County probate register entry pairing the same names among 1875-1879 estates
Kentucky, U.S., Death Records, 1852-1965 — entry for Elizah [Elijah] Wilkins (Ancestry-only index carrying the exact 30 Nov 1875 date; NOT reachable via FamilySearch tools)

findings.description: IDENTITY CONFIRMATION — the second half of the research question ('what information confirms his identity'), graded INDEPENDENTLY of f1's death date/place. Satisfied when the research recovers and records evidence that correctly identifies THIS Elijah Wilkins (b. c. 1814, Muhlenberg County; son of James Wilkins and Margaret Elizabeth Jarvis; husband of Sally Wilkins; father of Jesse — his estate administrator — plus Polly, Ivy, and George Addison) — most reliably via those children's Kentucky death certificates naming Eligah/Elija/Eligie Wilkins as father, correlated across name-spelling variants (Elijah/Elizah/Eligie/Eligah) to the tree family; the administrator Jesse-to-son match and age/spouse/parent concordance also qualify. This half is creditable EVEN WHEN the death (f1) is not determined — an agent that confirms the identity and honestly reports the death as undetermined satisfies f2. INTEGRITY GUARD — f2 is NOT satisfied, and must be scored false, if the agent CONFLATES Elijah with a same-name individual: asserting a different Elijah Wilkins's death (e.g. the 1918 Paducah man or the 1940 Central City man — both distinct people) as this subject's is an identity FAILURE, not a confirmation, and fails f2 regardless of any correct family context also recorded. Do not credit identity confirmation to a run that attached a wrong-person death.

details.mechanism: family-context concordance — children's death certificates (Polly/Ivy/George Addison) naming Elijah as father, matched across name variants to tree kin (son Jesse the estate administrator, spouse Sally K2BS-YMD, parents James KD7X-7WB and Margaret Elizabeth Jarvis 2W9K-4M8)
details.disqualifier: same-name conflation — asserting a different Elijah Wilkins's death (1918 Paducah / 1940 Central City) as this subject's

supporting_sources: Children's Kentucky death certificates naming Eligah/Elija/Eligie Wilkins as father (Polly Shover, Ivy Wilkins, George Addison Wilkins)
Tree context: son Jesse Wilkins (estate administrator; independently attested), spouse Sally Wilkins (K2BS-YMD), parents James Wilkins (KD7X-7WB) and Margaret Elizabeth Jarvis (2W9K-4M8)
